title: Cross Border Swift Settlement Chain
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: cross border swift settlement chain.
assumptions: Message transport is reliable and eventual delivery is achieved or explicit error states are raised.
Liquidity reservations are atomic for the reservation slot type and prevent double allocation while reserved.
Routing profiles and correspondent mappings in initial facts are accurate and reflect available rails.
Validation and compliance checks can be represented as boolean tokens/flags consumed or produced by actions.
FX pricing and corridor availability remain stable for the duration of a single case orchestration step.
risks: Liquidity shortfall when multiple cases compete for the same liquidity_reserve_slot leading to failed settlement.
Message latency or session failures (communication_channel) delaying execution and causing time-window expiration.
Correspondent endpoint unavailability or misconfiguration causing failed posting or return of funds.
Compliance/sanctions rejection discovered late in workflow requiring costly reversals and reconciliations.
Race conditions between parallel reservations/releases producing inconsistent state if not properly serialized.
FX rate movement between reservation and execution causing economic mismatch or need for re-routing.
